title: Rebuild is not recycled animation
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild is not "recycled animation"             Quote Post by V » Sat Oct 11, 2008 2:51 pm to this day, I am still seeing people saying that "Rebuild is just recycled animation" which leaves me openly surprised that they don't grasp how big of a remake it is many haven't actually seen it, and those that did were oddly unimpressed THE MOST WELL-BALANCED of reviews, of people who didn't like it, at least pointed out that "I didn't like this because it followed the first six episodes so closely (and thus "old" to them).....but I can tell from the twist ending that there are going to be major changes in the next movie so they were easing us into that, so I look forward to the next one, now that this "introduction" is out of the way" thoughts? why are so many people jumping to the conclusion that Rebuild is just "recycled animation"? and I don't just mean that I feel that the plot is different: I'm talking about how the animation quality has been DRASTICALLY improved, but there are reviewers who literally, LITERALLY think this is another Death&Rebirth with spliced in new animation mixed with old animation.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Oct 11, 2008 3:06 pm First rule of thumb -- people are idiots. From that, the rest follows.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sat Oct 11, 2008 3:30 pm There were on or two scenes that gave me an odd sense of deja-vu mind. Not just because it's basically a rethread of the first 6 episodes of the series now mind, but because some of the animation did look similar to parts of the original. If anything though, I was morereminded of EoE than of the original series, especially when the JSDF were pouring firepower onto the Angel. The VTOLS, the tanks, the multiple rocket launchers. They seem to be animated from a similar angle to those in EoE. I took a screenshot of one that stuck in my mind, reminding me of a similar shot in EoE. When I first saw this I wondered if maybe they handn't re-used one or two cells left over. It's probably just the camera angle and direction though mind... but here's thesnap http://i101.photobucket.com/albums/m57/Dartz_IRL/vlcsnap-3356.png

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Sat Oct 11, 2008 4:46 pm Are the military units still colored black? IF so that would be awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Oct 11, 2008 5:30 pm Mr. Tines wrote: First rule of thumb -- people are idiots. From that, the rest follows.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Sat Oct 11, 2008 7:56 pm Isn't there some kind of huge perspective problem on the picture posted above?

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sat Oct 11, 2008 8:32 pm V wrote: isn't that the Wizard's First Rule, or something? Randite platitudes, sir.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sat Oct 11, 2008 8:46 pm Some of the animation is basically a frame-by-frame trace of the original, but that's not to its detriment. The goal is to "rebuild" the series, after all, not make something that isn't Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sat Oct 11, 2008 8:47 pm I think it draws a few of the "recycled" footage accusations because certain scenes do seem to be almost exact duplicates of the original (such as Shinji and Misato at the table), but redrawn or touched up in some way. That being said, anybody with half a brain could recognize that Rebuild wasn't just a duplicate of the original story with prettier animation work.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Oct 11, 2008 10:22 pm @Drinian and C2: that's my point: they can make the criticism that "it wasn't that much different plot-wise" ...though I make the counter-argument that "they wanted to ease fans back into the series and make them accept that "we'll be changing stuff in this iconic show" by starting off almost exactly the same but gradually diverging i.e. Rebuild-Ramiel.... however, I have literally met people who think its another Death&Rebirth: a few new scenes spliced into what is literally decade-old animation. Heck, the scene of Toji punching Shinji was a shot-for-shot remake of the original scene, but the animation quality was obviously better and they obviously re-animated it from scratch...but nonetheless followed their old storyboards exactly.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sat Oct 11, 2008 11:19 pm The whiners will shut up when 2.0 comes out. I'd just ignore them. It's VERY obvious they re-animated the scenes and that it ain't no Death/Rebirth. Those people who think that must either a) be stupid or b) not have seen 1.0 at all.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Oct 12, 2008 12:20 am yeah that's also part of my point: you see the jaw-dropping trailer for Rebuild 2.0 at the end and you just go "my god, what the heck are they changing in this next one?!"

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sun Oct 12, 2008 4:05 am Anyone who says it's a complete re-tread is talking bollocks, just look at the Ramiel scene. What they've done is try and improve on what needed improving, with better animation and pacing. And since there was nothing wrong with the plot of the first 6 episodes why change it too much?

[]:             Quote Post by Zaiku » Sun Oct 12, 2008 4:18 am I read somewhere that they used the same storyboard for certain scenes. Obviously these people are dumb, as Rebuild has changed details such as the characters KNOWING that the angel on floor EEE is Lilith. Also, the preview at the end hints that things are going to be VERY different.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Sun Oct 12, 2008 11:00 am I don't think many of those people going about the "recycled animation" actually mean that they've just re-cut the old stuff à la Death - especially not for the entire Rebuild series, as most of these critics are just for 1.0 - but rather this whole tracing thing. Of course that's more work than they had for Death, but still relatively lazy compared to doing everything completely new. After all, there is quite a lot in 1.0 that's "just" touched up and giving it a consistent look compared to the mess the different studios made in the series. With camera angles, movements, timing, etc. being identical it might be shinier, but in the end we have already seen it, and I'd say these scenes outweigh the new ones in this movie. Likewise, the story for this movie is mostly a rehash of Episodes 1-6. That doesn't change just because it (maybe) makes a big turn in the next ones. As for those few guys though who seriously think it's just another Death with only the TV-Eps cut together.... I guess they don't give much damn about Eva in general. with better {..} pacing

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sun Oct 12, 2008 1:02 pm Slightly OT: was anyone else reminded of Webuild when watching the latest South Park episode? ;) Hopefully 2.0 won't give me recurring nightmares about Sadamoto and Anno raping Shinji. Anno: Well well well, were you waiting for us? Shinji: Well, the usual test was over, and I was going to have a shower before I went home. But, I don't want to go home these days. Anno: Pin him down, Yoshiyuki. Shinji: What?! Sadamoto: Yeah, touch his Nissan! Shinji: Help me, Asuka!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Oct 12, 2008 2:10 pm Whether you want to say it's "reworked", "renewed", "improved", or "recycled", it doesn't change the fact that it's not entirely new animation. There is new animation, but a lot of it has, in fact, been recycled.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sun Oct 12, 2008 2:26 pm How 'bout "rebuilt"?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Oct 12, 2008 3:40 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Whether you want to say it's "reworked", "renewed", "improved", or "recycled", it doesn't change the fact that it's not entirely new animation. There is new animation, but a lot of it has, in fact, been recycled.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Sun Oct 12, 2008 3:47 pm Hopefully 2.0 won't give me recurring nightmares about Sadamoto and Anno raping Shinji.
